title: Wireless Design, Configuration, and Validation · Protocol Clinic: Net Guest Captive
summary: Design staff, guest, and point-to-point wireless service across crowded, regulated, and mobile spaces. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net guest captive increment in a secure wireless survey and deployment plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design guest isolation and captive portals without confusing web acceptance with link-layer encryption or identity.
Choose WPA2 or WPA3 and PSK or enterprise authentication with migration, certificate, credential, and client constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Build Net Guest Captive evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-guest-captive-yvml42" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Guest Captive into observable performance evidence.
